def: Entreating; asking submissively. Shak.-- Sup"pli*cant*ly, adv.
Any one of numerous species of reptiles of the orderTestudinata.
A lady superior of a priory of nuns, and next in dignity to anabbess.
TO equal in weight; to counterpoise; to equiponderate.
Having the mandibles large and palmate, or branched somewhatlike the antlers of a stag; -- said of certain beetles.
Slightly clear; transmitting light in a slight degree.
One who marries a second time; a deuterogamist. Hammond.
Any long, slender, worm-shaped bivalve mollusk of Teredo andallied genera. The shipworms burrow in wood, and are destructive towooden ships, piles of wharves, etc. See Teredo.
The unlawful beating of another. It includes every willful,angry and violent, or negligent touching of another's person orclothes, or anything attached to his person or held by him.
